name: Service Map
description: Topology graph showing service dependencies with health indicators, animated traffic flow, and interactive detail panels for monitoring distributed service architectures.

graph.description: Force-directed graph layout where service nodes repel each other and edges act as springs — organic arrangement reveals natural clusters and heavily-connected hubs

hierarchy.description: Tree layout with entry-point services at the top and downstream dependencies flowing downward — reveals request flow direction and service depth in the call chain

grid.description: Organized grid layout where services are arranged in a structured matrix grouped by team or domain — edges route orthogonally between nodes for a clean architectural diagram feel

visual_brief: Force-directed graph rendered on a dark canvas where each service is a rounded rectangle node (approximately 120x60px) containing a small service type icon (database cylinder, server rack, cloud function, etc.), the service name in medium-weight text below the icon, and a small circular health dot in the top-right corner glowing green (healthy), yellow (degraded), or red (down). Curved bezier edges connect dependent services with animated dot particles flowing along the edge in the direction of requests. Edge thickness scales with traffic volume — thin hairlines for low-traffic dependencies, thick 4px lines for high-traffic paths. Hovering a node highlights all its direct dependency edges in the node's health color and fades all unrelated nodes and edges to 20% opacity. A detail panel slides in from the right side showing the selected service's key metrics: p50/p95/p99 latency as a mini histogram, error rate as a percentage with trend arrow, requests per second as a sparkline, and a list of direct upstream and downstream dependencies. A legend in the bottom-left corner explains health dot colors, edge thickness meaning, and node icon types. The hierarchy preset arranges nodes top-to-bottom with straight downward edges. The grid preset places nodes in labeled group boxes.

slots.map-canvas: The main rendering area occupying the full container. Supports pan (click-drag on background) and zoom (scroll wheel or pinch). Renders a subtle dot-grid background pattern for spatial reference. All service nodes and dependency edges are positioned within this canvas using absolute or SVG coordinates. The canvas auto-fits all nodes on initial load with padding.
slots.service-node: Repeating slot — one per service. A rounded rectangle (border-radius 12px) with a subtle border and semi-transparent background. Contains: a small icon representing the service type (16px), the service name (14px medium weight), and a HealthIndicator dot (8px circle) in the top-right corner. The node has a subtle drop shadow. On hover, the node elevates slightly and its border color intensifies to match its health status. Selected nodes show a ring highlight.
slots.dependency-edge: Repeating slot — one per service-to-service dependency. A curved SVG path connecting two service nodes. Animated dot particles (3px circles) travel along the path from caller to callee. Particle speed is proportional to request rate. Edge color defaults to a muted neutral but shifts to the caller's health color on hover. An optional small label at the midpoint shows request rate or protocol (REST, gRPC, etc.).
slots.detail-panel: Right-side panel (320px wide) that slides in when a service node is selected. Header shows service name, type badge, and health status with uptime percentage. Body contains: latency section with p50/p95/p99 values and a mini distribution chart, error rate with a small trend sparkline, throughput as requests/second with a sparkline, and a dependencies list showing upstream callers and downstream callees as clickable links that re-center the map on that service.
slots.legend: Small overlay card anchored to the bottom-left corner of the canvas. Shows: health dot color meanings (green=healthy, yellow=degraded, red=down), edge thickness scale (thin=low traffic, thick=high traffic), and node icon legend for service types.

responsive.mobile: Map canvas fills the screen with touch-based pan and pinch-to-zoom. Service nodes render at minimum size with name only (no icon). Detail panel opens as a bottom sheet instead of a side panel. Legend hides behind an info button. Edges simplified to straight lines without particles.
responsive.tablet: Standard force-directed layout with touch support. Detail panel slides in from the right at reduced width (280px). Service nodes show icon and name. Edge particles render at reduced density.
responsive.desktop: Full interactive map with hover effects, animated edge particles, and side detail panel. Zoom controls in the top-right corner. Legend always visible. Generous node sizing with icons, names, and health indicators.

micro.node-hover: scale(1→1.05) + elevation increase + border-color shift 150ms ease-out

transitions.layout-reflow: node positions lerp 600ms ease-in-out when switching presets

ambient.edge-particles: dot particles flowing along edges at speed proportional to request rate, continuous
ambient.health-pulse: scale(1→1.3→1) + opacity pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite on degraded/down health dots

composition.ServiceMap: Container(d-section, spatial) > [MapCanvas > ServiceNode*(d-interactive) + DependencyEdge*] + DetailPanel?(d-surface) + Legend(d-annotation)
composition.DetailPanel: Panel(d-surface, slide-right) > [Header > [ServiceName + TypeBadge + HealthStatus] + Metrics > [Latency + ErrorRate + Throughput] + DependencyList]

accessibility.aria-label: Service dependency map showing topology and health status
keyboard: Tab: cycle focus between service nodes in alphabetical order
Enter: select focused node and open detail panel
Escape: close detail panel and deselect node
Arrow keys: pan the map canvas
0: reset zoom to fit all nodes
